ED Raids Fuel Fresh Corruption Charges Against Karnataka Govt - BJP Claims 'Orga...
description: BJP leader R Ashoka has launched a fresh attack on the DK Shivakumar-led Congress government, alleging that ED raids exposed an 'organised syndicate' in Karnataka's Excise Department.
content: BJP leader and Leader of the Opposition in the Karnataka Assembly R Ashoka has launched a sharp attack on the DK Shivakumar-led Congress government, alleging that the Enforcement Directorate's recent raids had exposed an "organised syndicate" operating within the state's Excise Department.  Related News | Not A Technical Fault: BMRCL Reveals What Caused Bengaluru Purple Line Delay—It Was A Metal ChainIn a post on social media, Ashoka accused the Shivakumar government of presiding over rampant corruption and claimed that nearly every department had become a centre of graft since the Congress came to power in Karnataka."There is no end to the Congress government's loot, there is no end to the chain of scams!"A scandal every day, a naked glimpse of loot every day! Since the @INCKarnataka government came to power in Karnataka, every department has become a hotbed of corruption.  Related News | Bengaluru Footpath Crackdown From July 1: Encroachments To Be Removed Across 2,000 Km Of Roads"As revealed in a press report, the Enforcement Directorate (ED) has unearthed the fact that the Congress government is leading an "organized syndicate" in the Excise Department just to collect bribes!"This is not just corruption, it is a systematic robbery going on with the blessings of influential people in the government!"Ashoka then went on to reveal the "naked truths" in the excise scam. He claimed that black money and gold worth ₹13.3 crore had been recovered from Belagavi Division Additional Commissioner YD Manjunath and his associates. He further alleged that investigators had seized a "secret book" containing records of bribe collections, including details of who paid the money and where it was allegedly routed. He also accused officials of obtaining benami bar licences in the names of family members and close associates, claiming that the practice had compromised the integrity of the licensing system.  Related News | Bengaluru Traffic Alert: Muharram Procession to Affect Key Routes on Saturday; Check Diversions"Naked truths revealed in the Excise Scam: The Minister's close ties: Public Works Minister Satish Jarkiholi's own brother, Belagavi Division Additional Commissioner YD, Black money and gold worth ₹13.3 crore have been recovered from Manjunath and his associates!"The ‘master book’ of bribery accounts: How much has been collected from whom? To whom has that money reached? The ‘secret bribery book’ with every inch of information has now reached the hands of ED officials!"Benami bar license scam: Officials themselves are getting benami licenses in the names of their family members and close friends and are swallowing the entire system."  Related News | 450 Days Of Protest, 4,500 Objections: JDS' Kumaraswamy Accepts Debate On Bidadi Township ProjectAshoka then said that ED officials had conducted simultaneous searches at 14 locations across Karnataka as part of the probe, exposing what he described as a large-scale scam within the Excise Department. "ED officials have simultaneously raided 14 important places in the state and exposed the color of this cosmic scam."Targeting Chief Minister Shivakumar, Ashoka questioned how deep the alleged corruption network extended within the government. "CM @DKShivakumar If you see that the families of influential ministers in your cabinet are leading this cosmic bribery avatar, it becomes clear how far the roots of this collection scam have gone."- Did you also get your share of the money recorded in this ‘secret book’?- Did the money from the monthly commission scam reach the Delhi High Command?- Who is the real father of this organized syndicate that is soaked in the blood of the people of the state?"  Related News | Actor Krishi Thapanda’s Friend Found Dead in Her Apartment in BengaluruCalling for a thorough probe, the BJP leader demanded that the case be handed over to the CBI or to a judicial commission headed by a High Court judge. "How long will you remain silent even though the loot of one department after another of your government is being exposed? The people of Karnataka will never forgive your commission rule. This scam should be immediately handed over to a judicial investigation led by a High Court judge or the CBI, and those who have tarnished the ministerial post should be punished. #CongressLootsKarnataka"The Karnataka government has not immediately responded to Ashoka's latest allegations.

title: ‘Let market forces decide bancassurance partnerships, IRDAI will focus on policyholder protection’: Swaminathan S. Iyer
description: In an exclusive interaction with ET Now’s Insurance Mantra, Iyer said the regulator’s role is to create an enabling framework for orderly market growth rather than dictate commercial decisions.
content: The Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (IRDAI) is unlikely to prescribe how banks should structure their insurance partnerships, preferring to let market forces determine business models while keeping policyholder protection at the heart of upcoming distribution reforms, according to Swaminathan S. Iyer, Member (Life), IRDAI.In an exclusive interaction with ET Now’s Insurance Mantra, Iyer said the regulator’s role is to create an enabling framework for orderly market growth rather than dictate commercial decisions. His remarks come as the insurance industry awaits IRDAI’s much-anticipated discussion paper on distribution reforms, which is expected to address commissions, distributor remuneration, persistency and customer protection.Clarifying IRDAI’s stance on bancassurance and open architecture, Iyer said banks are already free to partner with multiple insurers under the existing regulatory framework and many institutions have voluntarily adopted such models.“Regulations are open today. Some banks and corporations have gone to multiple partnerships and are doing an equally good job. Others have consciously chosen not to. We leave it to market forces,” he said.He stressed that IRDAI does not intend to interfere with commercial business models merely for the sake of regulation.“As a regulator, we do not want to get into the way businesses are run by intermediaries or insurers. We want to enable orderly growth in the industry, with policyholder protection as our primary focus,” Iyer said.With India aiming to achieve the goal of ‘Insurance for All by 2047,’ Iyer said expanding the distribution network remains a national priority.Related News | NRE or NRO? How NRIs can save lakhs in taxes on interest income - All you need to know“This country needs many more distribution channels. If we have to reach over 1.4 billion people today and an even larger population by 2047, we need multiple distribution models,” he said.On the long-awaited distribution reforms, Iyer confirmed that the regulator is actively working on the framework but said the final shape of the regulations would depend on stakeholder consultations.“The discussion paper will come out soon. It all depends on the feedback we receive from the public as well as the industry because this is a large-impact area,” he said.The proposed reforms come against the backdrop of concerns raised by the RBI, the Economic Survey and the Department of Financial Services over rising distribution costs and commissions. However, Iyer refrained from commenting on specific proposals, noting that the exercise remains a work in progress.“As a regulatory authority, our focus will always remain the protection of policyholders’ interests,” he said.According to Iyer, future commission structures may not follow a uniform formula. Instead, they could reflect the effort involved in selling different insurance products.“Product complexity, simplicity, policy tenure, customer profile and persistency are all factors that may be linked while examining distribution structures,” he said.He pointed out that several insurers have already begun linking distributor incentives to policy persistency after earlier regulatory changes.“Some insurers have already given greater weightage to persistency while rewarding distributors. We will certainly study all these developments,” he said.On the issue of mis-selling, Iyer maintained that it is not confined to any single distribution channel, including banks. Rather, he believes the root cause is inadequate disclosure of product information.“The customer is the king in a free-market economy, and the king has the right to complete information before making a decision,” he said.He added that transparent disclosure significantly reduces the possibility of mis-selling.“Once complete information is shared with the customer, the probability of mis-selling comes down substantially,” Iyer observed.Addressing the industry’s protection gap, he said term insurance continues to present a unique distribution challenge because it requires greater selling effort despite lower premium sizes.“Selling a term plan is more challenging because customers often ask, ‘What do I get back?’ The intermediary needs adequate incentives because selling protection is the right thing to do for the customer,” he said.Iyer also underscored the importance of policy persistency, saying keeping customers protected throughout the policy term is as important as acquiring new business.https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CZz1BYjf5o4“Policyholders who drop out of the system need to be brought back. Persistency is an area where the entire industry must work collectively,” he said.Summing up the regulator’s philosophy, Iyer reiterated that future reforms would seek to strike a balance between industry growth, market flexibility and customer protection.“We will not move towards overly prescriptive regulations. Our objective is sustainable industry growth while ensuring that policyholders remain fully protected,” he said.The forthcoming distribution reforms are expected to be among the most significant regulatory changes for India’s insurance sector in recent years, with implications for insurers, banks, intermediaries and millions of policyholders.

title: ନିଜ କାର୍ଯ୍ୟକୁ ଫେରିଲେ ସରପଞ୍ଚ ଚାମେଲୀ ଓଝା, ପଞ୍ଚାୟତର ଉନ୍ନୟନ କାର୍ଯ୍ୟର ସମୀକ୍ଷା କରି…
description:

The post ନିଜ କାର୍ଯ୍ୟକୁ ଫେରିଲେ ସରପଞ୍ଚ ଚାମେଲୀ ଓଝା, ପଞ୍ଚାୟତର ଉନ୍ନୟନ କାର୍ଯ୍ୟର ସମୀକ୍ଷା କରି… appeared first on Dharitri Odia News.
content:
ମହାକାଳପଡ଼ା, ୨୬ା୬(ପ୍ରତାପ ଚନ୍ଦ୍ର ସ୍ବାଇଁ): କେନ୍ଦ୍ରାପଡ଼ା ଜିଲା ମହାକାଳପଡା ବ୍ଲକ ତିଖିରୀ ପଞ୍ଚାୟତର ଉନ୍ନୟନମୂଳକ କାର୍ଯ୍ୟକୁ ରୋକାଯିବା ଓ କରାଯାଇଥିବା କାମର ବିଲ୍‌ ପ୍ରଦାନ କରାଯାଉ ନ ଥିବା ଅଭିଯୋଗ କରିଥିଲେ ସରପଞ୍ଚ ଚାମେଲୀ ଓଝା। ଏହି ଅଭିଯୋଗ ନେଇ ଗତ ୧୧ତାରିଖରେ ସରପଞ୍ଚ ଚାମେଲୀ ବ୍ଲକ ଅଫିସ ଯାଇଥିବାବେଳେ ବିଡିଓଙ୍କ ଚ୍ୟାମ୍ବରରେ ବିବାଦୀୟ ପରିସ୍ଥିତି ସୃଷ୍ଟି ହୋଇଥିଲା। ଚାମ୍ବରରେ ଭିଡିଓ ରେକର୍ଡିଂ ଓ ଥାନାକୁ ନେବାବେଳେ ପୋଲିସ କର୍ମଚାରୀଙ୍କୁ ଆକ୍ରମଣ କରିବା ଅଭିଯୋଗରେ ଚାମେଲୀଙ୍କୁ ଜେଲ ଯିବାକୁ ପଡିଥିଲା। ଏହାକୁ ନେଇ ରାଜ୍ୟବ୍ୟାପୀ ହଇଚଇ ସୃଷ୍ଟି ହୋଇଥିଲା। ଏବେ ଏହି ଘଟଣାର ମୂଳ ସମସ୍ୟାକୁ ଦୂର କରିବାକୁ ପ୍ରଶାସନ ପ୍ରକ୍ରିୟା ଆରମ୍ଭ କରିଛି। ଗୁରୁବାର ପୂର୍ବାହ୍ନରେ ବ୍ଲକ ସଭା ଗୃହଠାରେ ପଞ୍ଚାୟତ ଉନ୍ନୟନମୂଳକ କାର୍ଯ୍ୟ ଉପରେ ଆଲୋଚନା ହୋଇଥିଲା। ଏଥିରେ ଅତିରିକ୍ତ ଜିଲାପାଳ ରବିନ୍ଦ୍ର କୁମାର ମଲିକ, ଜିଲା ମୁଖ୍ୟ ଉନ୍ନୟନ ଅଧିକାରୀ ତଥା ପ୍ରକଳ୍ପ ନିର୍ଦ୍ଦେଶକ ଅଜାମ୍ବର ମହାନ୍ତି, ସହକାରୀ ଜିଲାପାଳ ସରୋଜ କୁମାର ବେହେରା, ଜିଲା ପରିଷଦ ସହକାରୀ ନିର୍ବାହୀ ଯନ୍ତ୍ରୀ ଦେବଯାନୀ ସୂତାର, ଏପିଡି ମନୀଷା ଗୁପ୍ତା ପ୍ରମୁଖ ଉପସ୍ଥିତ ରହି ସରପଞ୍ଚ ଓଝାଙ୍କ ସହିତ ପଞ୍ଚାୟତ ଉନ୍ନୟନ କାର୍ଯ୍ୟ ଉପରେ ଆଲୋଚନା କରିଥିଲେ। ପୂର୍ବରୁ କରାଯାଇଥିବା କାର୍ଯ୍ୟର କେତେକ ବକେୟା ପାଉଣା ରହିଛି ତାହା ଉପରେ ଗୁରୁତ୍ୱାରୋପ କରାଯାଇଥିଲା। ସେହିପରି ପଞ୍ଚାୟତରେ ୪୩ ଲକ୍ଷ ଟଙ୍କାର କାର୍ଯ୍ୟ ଶେଷ ହୋଇଥିବା ଆଲୋଚନାରୁ ଜଣାପଡିଥିଲା। ପୂର୍ବତନ ବିଧାୟକଙ୍କ ଲ୍ୟାଡରୁ ୧୫ ଲକ୍ଷ ଟଙ୍କା ରହିଥିବା ସମୀକ୍ଷାରୁ ଜଣାପଡିଛି। କେତେକ କାର୍ଯ୍ୟରେ ବିଲ୍‌ ଅନୁମୋଦନ ହୋଇଥିଲାବେଳେ ଆଉ କେତେକ ବିଲ୍‌ ଅଟକି ଥିବା ସରପଞ୍ଚ ଓଝା କହିଥିଲେ। ଅନ୍ୟପକ୍ଷରେ ଏହି ବିବାଦୀୟ ଘଟଣା ଉପରେ ଜିଲାପାଳ ରଘୁରାମ ଆର ଆୟର ତିଖିରୀ ପଞ୍ଚାୟତରେ ଉନ୍ନୟନମୂଳକ କାର୍ଯ୍ୟର ଅଗ୍ରଗତି, ବକେୟା ପାଉଣା ବାବଦରେ ଏକ ୫ ଜଣିଆ କମିଟି ଗଠନ କରିଛନ୍ତି ବୋଲି ଜିଲାସ୍ତରୀୟ ଅଧିକାରୀମାନେ କହିଛନ୍ତି। ତେବେ ତାଙ୍କୁ ଦୁର୍ବ୍ୟବହାର କରିଥିବା ବ୍ୟକ୍ତିମାନେ ଗିରଫ ନ ହେବା ପର୍ଯ୍ୟନ୍ତ ରାଜ୍ୟପାଳଙ୍କୁ ଭେଟିବି ବୋଲି ଓଝା କହିଛନ୍ତି।

Dharitri – Odisha’s No.1 Odia Daily

title: ଡେବ୍ୟୁ କରିବା ମାତ୍ରେ ଭାରତୀୟ କ୍ରିକେଟ ଇତିହାସ ବଦଳାଇଦେବେ ବୈଭବ ସୂର୍ଯ୍ୟବଂଶୀ: ଭାଙ୍ଗିବେ ୫ ମହାରେକର୍ଡ
description:
ନୂଆଦିଲ୍ଲୀ,୨୬।୬:ଭାରତୀୟ କ୍ରିକେଟ ଦଳର ୧୫ ବର୍ଷୀୟ ବ୍ୟାଟସମ୍ୟାନ ବୈଭବ ସୂର୍ଯ୍ୟବଂଶୀ ଚର୍ଚ୍ଚାର ବିଷୟ ପାଲଟିଛନ୍ତି। ସେ ଆୟରଲାଣ୍ଡ ବିପକ୍ଷରେ ଆଗାମୀ ଟି-୨୦ ସିରିଜ ପାଇଁ ବରିଷ୍ଠ ଭାରତୀୟ ଦଳରେ ସ୍ଥାନ ପାଇଛନ୍ତି। ଏହି ସିରିଜର ପ୍ରଥମ ମ୍ୟାଚ ଆଜି ଅନୁଷ୍ଠିତ ହେବ। ଯଦି ବୈଭବଙ୍କୁ ଭାରତର ପ୍ଲେଇଂ ଇଲେଭେନରେ ସାମିଲ କରାଯାଏ, ତେବେ ସେ ପଡ଼ିଆକୁ ଓହ୍ଲାଇବା ମାତ୍ରେ ‘କ୍ରିକେଟ ର ଭଗବାନ’ ସଚିନ ତେନ୍ଦୁଲକରଙ୍କଠାରୁ ଆରମ୍ଭ କରି ବର୍ତ୍ତମାନର ପ୍ରଖ୍ୟାତ ଖେଳାଳି ରୋହିତ ଶର୍ମାଙ୍କ ପର୍ଯ୍ୟନ୍ତ […]
The post ଡେବ୍ୟୁ କରିବା ମାତ୍ରେ ଭାରତୀୟ କ୍ରିକେଟ ଇତିହାସ ବଦଳାଇଦେବେ ବୈଭବ ସୂର୍ଯ୍ୟବଂଶୀ: ଭାଙ୍ଗିବେ ୫ ମହାରେକର୍ଡ appeared first on Dharitri Odia News.
content:
ନୂଆଦିଲ୍ଲୀ,୨୬।୬:ଭାରତୀୟ କ୍ରିକେଟ ଦଳର ୧୫ ବର୍ଷୀୟ ବ୍ୟାଟସମ୍ୟାନ ବୈଭବ ସୂର୍ଯ୍ୟବଂଶୀ ଚର୍ଚ୍ଚାର ବିଷୟ ପାଲଟିଛନ୍ତି। ସେ ଆୟରଲାଣ୍ଡ ବିପକ୍ଷରେ ଆଗାମୀ ଟି-୨୦ ସିରିଜ ପାଇଁ ବରିଷ୍ଠ ଭାରତୀୟ ଦଳରେ ସ୍ଥାନ ପାଇଛନ୍ତି। ଏହି ସିରିଜର ପ୍ରଥମ ମ୍ୟାଚ ଆଜି ଅନୁଷ୍ଠିତ ହେବ। ଯଦି ବୈଭବଙ୍କୁ ଭାରତର ପ୍ଲେଇଂ ଇଲେଭେନରେ ସାମିଲ କରାଯାଏ, ତେବେ ସେ ପଡ଼ିଆକୁ ଓହ୍ଲାଇବା ମାତ୍ରେ ‘କ୍ରିକେଟ ର ଭଗବାନ’ ସଚିନ ତେନ୍ଦୁଲକରଙ୍କଠାରୁ ଆରମ୍ଭ କରି ବର୍ତ୍ତମାନର ପ୍ରଖ୍ୟାତ ଖେଳାଳି ରୋହିତ ଶର୍ମାଙ୍କ ପର୍ଯ୍ୟନ୍ତ ଅନେକ ଐତିହାସିକ ରେକର୍ଡ ଭାଙ୍ଗି ପାରିବେ।
ପୁରୁଷ ଅନ୍ତର୍ଜାତୀୟ କ୍ରିକେଟରେ ଡେବ୍ୟୁ କରିଥିବା ସର୍ବକନିଷ୍ଠ ଭାରତୀୟ ଖେଳାଳିଙ୍କ ରେକର୍ଡ କିମ୍ବଦନ୍ତୀ ସଚିନ ତେନ୍ଦୁଲକରଙ୍କର। ସଚିନ ୧୯୮୯ ମସିହାରେ ମାତ୍ର ୧୬ ବର୍ଷ ୨୦୫ ଦିନ ବୟସରେ ପାକିସ୍ତାନ ବିପକ୍ଷରେ ତାଙ୍କର ପ୍ରଥମ ମ୍ୟାଚ ଖେଳିଥିଲେ। ଯଦି ବୈଭବ ଆୟରଲାଣ୍ଡ ବିପକ୍ଷରେ ତାଙ୍କର ଟି-୨୦ କ୍ୟାପ ପାଆନ୍ତି, ତେବେ ସେ ୧୫ ବର୍ଷ ବୟସରେ ପଡ଼ିଆକୁ ଓହ୍ଲାଇବେ, ଯାହା ଦ୍ୱାରା ସଚିନଙ୍କ ୩୭ ବର୍ଷର ପୁରୁଣା ଐତିହାସିକ ରେକର୍ଡ ଭାଙ୍ଗି ଭାରତର ସର୍ବକନିଷ୍ଠ ଅନ୍ତର୍ଜାତୀୟ କ୍ରିକେଟର ହେବେ।
ଭାରତ ପାଇଁ ଡେବ୍ୟୁ ଟି-୨୦ ଇନିଂସରେ ସର୍ବାଧିକ ଛକା ମାରିବାର ରେକର୍ଡ ଉଇକେଟକିପର-ବ୍ୟାଟସମ୍ୟାନ ଇଶାନ କିଶାନଙ୍କ ନାମରେ ଅଛି। ଇଶାନ ଅହମ୍ମଦାବାଦରେ ଇଂଲଣ୍ଡ ବିପକ୍ଷରେ ତାଙ୍କର ଡେବ୍ୟୁ ମ୍ୟାଚ୍‌ରେ ଚାରୋଟି ଉଚ୍ଚ ଛକା ମାରିଥିଲେ। ତାଙ୍କର ଆକ୍ରମଣାତ୍ମକ ଏବଂ ସଫା ହିଟିଂ ପାଇଁ ଜଣାଶୁଣା ବୈଭବ ସୂର୍ଯ୍ୟବଂଶୀ, ଆୟରଲାଣ୍ଡ ବିପକ୍ଷରେ ତାଙ୍କର ଡେବ୍ୟୁ ମ୍ୟାଚ୍‌ରେ ପାଞ୍ଚଟି ଛକା ମାରିଲେ ଇଶାନ କିଶାନଙ୍କୁ ପଛରେ ପକାଇ ଏହି ତାଲିକାର ଶୀର୍ଷରେ ପହଞ୍ଚିପାରିବେ।
ଡେବ୍ୟୁ ଟି-୨୦ ଅନ୍ତର୍ଜାତୀୟ ମ୍ୟାଚ୍‌ରେ ଜଣେ ଭାରତୀୟଙ୍କ ଦ୍ୱାରା ସର୍ବାଧିକ ସ୍କୋର କରିବାର ରେକର୍ଡ ଅଭିଜ୍ଞ ବ୍ୟାଟସମ୍ୟାନ ଅଜିଙ୍କ୍ୟ ରାହାଣେଙ୍କ ନାମରେ ଅଛି। ୨୦୧୧ରେ, ରାହାଣେ ଇଂଲଣ୍ଡ ବିପକ୍ଷରେ ତାଙ୍କର ପ୍ରଥମ ଟି-୨୦ ମ୍ୟାଚ୍‌ରେ ୩୯ ବଲ୍‌ରେ ୬୧ ରନ୍‌ର ଏକ ଚମତ୍କାର ଏବଂ ବିସ୍ଫୋରକ ଇନିଂସ ଖେଳିଥିଲେ। ଯଦି ବୈଭବ ସୂର୍ଯ୍ୟବଂଶୀ ଆୟରଲାଣ୍ଡ ବିପକ୍ଷରେ ତାଙ୍କର ଡେବ୍ୟୁ ମ୍ୟାଚ୍‌ରେ ୬୨ ରନ କରିବାକୁ ସଫଳ ହୁଅନ୍ତି, ତେବେ ସେ ରାହାଣେଙ୍କ ୧୫ ବର୍ଷ ପୁରୁଣା ରେକର୍ଡକୁ ମଧ୍ୟ ଇତିହାସ ପୁସ୍ତକରେ ସ୍ଥାନିତ କରିବେ।
ଭାରତୀୟ କ୍ରିକେଟ ଇତିହାସରେ, ଏପର୍ଯ୍ୟନ୍ତ ଚାରି ଜଣ ଖେଳାଳି ଆୟର୍ଲାଣ୍ଡ ମାଟିରେ ଟି-୨୦ ଆନ୍ତର୍ଜାତୀୟ ଡେବ୍ୟୁ କରିଛନ୍ତି: ସିଦ୍ଧାର୍ଥ କାଉଲ, ଉମରାନ ମଲିକ, ପ୍ରସିଦ୍ଧ କ୍ରିଷ୍ଣା ଏବଂ ରିଙ୍କୁ ସିଂହ। ଆଶ୍ଚର୍ଯ୍ୟଜନକ ଭାବରେ ଏହି ଖେଳାଳିମାନଙ୍କ ମଧ୍ୟରୁ କେହି ବି ଡେବ୍ୟୁ ମ୍ୟାଚ୍‌ରେ ଗୋଟିଏ ବି ରନ କରିପାରି ନାହାଁନ୍ତି। ଯଦି ବୈଭବ ଆୟର୍ଲାଣ୍ଡରେ ଡେବ୍ୟୁ କରିବାର ସୁଯୋଗ ପାଆନ୍ତି ଏବଂ ଗୋଟିଏ ରନ ମଧ୍ୟ ସ୍କୋର କରନ୍ତି, ତେବେ ସେ ଆୟର୍ଲାଣ୍ଡ ମାଟିରେ ଡେବ୍ୟୁ କରିବାରେ ପ୍ରଥମ ଭାରତୀୟ ଡେବ୍ୟୁ ଖେଳାଳି ହେବେ।
ରୋହିତ ଶର୍ମା ଆଇର୍ଲାଣ୍ଡ ପିଚ୍‌ରେ ଟି-୨୦ କ୍ରିକେଟ୍‌ରେ ସବୁଠାରୁ ସଫଳ ଭାରତୀୟ ଓପନର୍ସ। ସେ ଆଇର୍ଲାଣ୍ଡ ମାଟିରେ ୧୫୯.୦୧ ର ଏକ ଧମାକାଦାର ଷ୍ଟ୍ରାଇକ ରେଟ୍‌ରେ ମୋଟ ୯୭ ରନ କରିଛନ୍ତି। ଯଦି ବୈଭବ ସୂର୍ଯ୍ୟବଂଶୀ ତାଙ୍କର ପ୍ରଥମ ମ୍ୟାଚ୍‌ରେ ଓପନିଂ ସମୟରେ ୯୮ ରନ୍‌ର ଏକ ଯାଦୁକରୀ ଇନିଂସ ଖେଳିବେ, ତେବେ ସେ ରୋହିତ ଶର୍ମାଙ୍କୁ ପଛରେ ପକାଇ ଆୟର୍ଲାଣ୍ଡରେ ସର୍ବାଧିକ ରନ ସହିତ ଭାରତୀୟ ଟି-୨୦ ଓପନର୍‌ ହେବେ।
 

Dharitri – Odisha’s No.1 Odia Daily

 
The post ଡେବ୍ୟୁ କରିବା ମାତ୍ରେ ଭାରତୀୟ କ୍ରିକେଟ ଇତିହାସ ବଦଳାଇଦେବେ ବୈଭବ ସୂର୍ଯ୍ୟବଂଶୀ: ଭାଙ୍ଗିବେ ୫ ମହାରେକର୍ଡ appeared first on Dharitri Odia News.

title: Darshan's wife dismisses son Vineesh accident rumours
description: Vijayalakshmi Darshan, wife of jailed actor Darshan Thoogudeepa, has debunked viral rumors about their son Vineesh being hospitalized after a car accident. She clarified that Vineesh was safe, not driving, and only checked on a two-wheeler rider after a minor collision in Bengaluru. Police have confirmed no case was registered for the incident, which has caused distress to the family already facing public scrutiny.

title: Katy takes a dig at Orlando in 'Watch It Burn' video
description: Katy Perry has unleashed her fiery new music video for 'Watch It Burn,' showcasing a dramatic shift to a darker persona. The visuals, packed with symbolic 'easter eggs' hinting at past relationships, depict her embracing anger and moving on from past hurts. This powerful release coincides with her blossoming romance with Justin Trudeau, marking a new chapter for the pop star.

Pharmeasy founders’ new venture raises Rs 200 cr
Google Finance: Tech giant forays into fintech market, launches dedicated app; Yahoo finance, Robinhood major competitors
description: Google has finally stepped into Fintech game, launching its dedicated finance app, entering fintech market, now eyeing to compete with Yahoo finance and Robinhood.
content: In a significant move to strengthen its presence in the financial technology space, Google has officially launched a standalone Google Finance app for Android users. Announced on June 25, 2026, the new app brings together watchlists, real-time market data, live financial news, and innovative AI features into a single, user-friendly mobile experience.What is ‘Key Moments’The app's centerpiece is Google’s AI-powered “Key Moments” feature, which delivers clear explanations for stock movements, helping both novice and experienced investors quickly understand market dynamics without sifting through dense reports. Users can access their personalized watchlists on the go, receive live updates, and stay informed with curated news feeds. Google has confirmed that an iOS version is in development and expected in the coming months, along with additional features like the ability to listen to live earnings calls.Yahoo Finance and Robinhood are major competitorsThis launch represents more than just a new place to check stock prices, industry observers see it as Google’s strategic bid to claim a larger share of the crowded financial information and investment app market. The company is positioning itself against established players such as Yahoo Finance and popular trading platforms like Robinhood. By leveraging its vast data resources and advanced AI capabilities, Google aims to offer a more intelligent and integrated experience than its competitors.Google also rolling ‘Finance Web’Alongside the mobile app, Google is rolling out major enhancements to its Google Finance web platform, which recently exited beta. Users worldwide can now access comprehensive portfolios in a unified dashboard that tracks holdings and performance in real time. Existing portfolios migrate automatically, while new ones can be created easily either by uploading files or simply describing investments to Google’s AI chatbot.The AI research tool allows interactive queries, such as “What sectors are currently underrepresented in my portfolio?” This conversational approach makes portfolio management more accessible. Furthermore, users can set up natural-language tasks for automated briefings on market changes or performance summaries. Once configured, these tasks run in the background, delivering timely insights tailored to individual investments. These web features will gradually make their way to the mobile app.The timing of Google’s push into dedicated finance tools reflects broader trends in consumer fintech. With more retail investors seeking sophisticated yet simple tools, the integration of AI for analysis and personalization could prove decisive. Google’s deep ecosystem integration linking search, news, and now finance—gives it unique advantages in delivering context-aware information.For Android users, the Google Finance app is available now, marking an important step in Google’s evolution from a search giant to a multifaceted technology provider. As the company continues to expand AI across its products, the finance vertical stands out as a natural extension where data intelligence can deliver immediate user value.Analysts predict that success will depend on Google’s ability to build trust in financial data handling while differentiating through seamless AI experiences. Early feedback suggests the combination of real-time data, explanatory AI, and portfolio tools could resonate strongly with users looking for an all-in-one solution. With iOS support on the horizon and more features planned, Google Finance is poised to become a major contender in mobile investing and market intelligence.

title: 'Too Late Now,' Siya Claims Ketan Said To Her, 'Didn't Want To Marry'
description: Siya Goyal now claims she told Ketan Agrawal she didn't want to marry him and that he refused to end the engagement, but police say chats recovered from their phones show no sign of this and instead reveal an affectionate, normal couple.
content: Pune: In a fresh twist in the Lohagad Fort murder case, accused Siya Goyal has reportedly given investigators a new version of events, one that police sources say doesn't match the evidence on her own phone.According to Lonavala Police sources, Siya told investigators during questioning that she had informed Ketan Agrawal she did not want to marry  him, and that Ketan had responded saying it was "too late now," and that everything had gone too far, so nothing could be changed.But that account is running into trouble. Police sources said investigators have seized both Siya's and Ketan's mobile phones, and the chats recovered from them appear to tell a very different story, one of affectionate, ordinary conversations between a couple preparing for their wedding, with no sign of the resistance or conflict Siya has now described.That mismatch, sources say, has left police unconvinced by her latest claim at this stage of the investigation. Officers are continuing to examine the case from multiple angles, cross-referencing statements against digital evidence as the probe progresses.  3 Failed Attempts, A Lost Passport And The Final PushThe development adds to an already complex picture in a case where police allege Siya and her co-accused, Chetan Chaudhary, made four attempts to kill Ketan before succeeding on 18 June, and where the two are now also reportedly blaming each other over who delivered the fatal push at the fort.The first attempt was allegedly made on 31 May, when Siya took Ketan to the fort intending to push him from a deserted point and pass it off as an accidental fall during a trek. According to police sources, Siya later told investigators she chose Lohagad specifically because the isolated spot made it easy to disguise a fatal fall as a trekking accident, exploiting Ketan's own love for trekking as the lure.That plan failed, and Siya and Chetan allegedly regrouped on 1 June to draw up what they considered a "foolproof" second attempt for 4 June. That collapsed too, partly because of an upcoming Bali trip scheduled for 6 June. Police sources say Chetan had been pressuring Siya to keep Ketan away from that trip, and under that pressure, Siya allegedly tore up Ketan's passport and flushed it  down a hotel washroom en route to the airport on 6 June, using a missing-phone excuse to create a window to destroy the document. The Bali trip was cancelled, and Ketan, suspicious that the passport hadn't simply gone missing, reportedly got into repeated arguments with Siya in the days that followed.A third attempt followed on 14 June, when Siya again took Ketan to the fort, but the push failed after he got caught in a bush. When confronted, she allegedly claimed she had pushed him because she'd spotted a snake.It was this third failure, sources say, that pushed Chetan past his limit. He is alleged to have told Siya: "Ab naya plan 18 tareekh ka hai... Ketan ko main maarunga, tumse nahi ho payega" (the new plan is for the 18th... I'll kill Ketan, you won't be able to do it). For the first three attempts, Chetan had reportedly stayed away from the fort altogether; for 18 June, he decided to go to Lohagad himself for the first time and carry out the murder personally.

title: Are You An Indian? The Passport That Doesn't Settle The Question
description: The government's clarification that a passport is not conclusive proof of citizenship has left many Indians asking a simple question: if not a passport, then what is?
content: For most Indians, the passport has always felt like the gold standard. It is the document we guard most carefully. It gets stored inside lockers and is treated with far more respect than most government papers. It even survives floods in plastic folders. After all, it is issued only after thorough, multi-level checks... our documents are scrutinised, our addresses verified and the process culminates in a police verification at our doorstep. In other words, a passport is issued only after multiple layers of due diligence.So when the government recently 'reminded' us that it is merely a travel document and not conclusive proof of Indian citizenship, it left many asking a simple question: If not a passport, then what exactly proves that I am an Indian citizen?The irony is hard to miss. The same passport is accepted almost everywhere else. Want a driving licence? Passport works. Opening a bank account? Passport works. Applying for a PAN card? Passport works. Getting a gas connection? Passport works. It sits among the highest-value documents recognised by the government itself. That is what has made this debate so confusing. Open the first page of an Indian passport and it carries the words "By Order of the President of the Republic of India". For most citizens, that has always felt like the state's ultimate endorsement of who they are. The blue cover tells the world you are Indian... just apparently not conclusively to India. The government's answer is rooted in law. It says citizenship and passports are governed by two different legislations. Citizenship is determined under the Citizenship Act, while passports  are issued under the Passports Act. In other words, a passport allows you to travel internationally, but if your citizenship is ever challenged in a court of law, the passport alone cannot settle that question.Legally, there is logic to this position. The Passports Act gives authorities the power to refuse a passport if an applicant is not an Indian citizen. At the same time, another provision allows the government, in exceptional public interest situations, to issue travel documents aka passport to certain categories of non-citizens, such as stateless persons or other exceptional cases. Because this exception exists, the government argues that a passport cannot be treated as conclusive proof of citizenship.  That explanation may satisfy lawyers. It has left many ordinary Indians bewildered.Because in everyday life, the passport enjoys a status few other documents do. It is accepted to open bank accounts, obtain driving licences, apply for PAN cards and complete countless official formalities. It tells foreign governments that you are Indian. Yet, if your citizenship itself is questioned, it suddenly becomes insufficient.This debate also revives memories of moments when citizenship became a source of anxiety. During the NRC exercise in Assam, thousands struggled to establish their citizenship despite possessing multiple government documents. More recently, during SIR, documentation once again became a contentious issue. These episodes exposed an uncomfortable truth: India has plenty of identity documents, but no single citizenship document.When Aadhaar was introduced, it was seen as the one document that would simplify identity in India. Today, Aadhaar enrolment and updates happen at banks, post offices and authorised centres across the country and it is seen as a document that's easily attainable. The government has also repeatedly clarified that Aadhaar is proof of identity and residence, not citizenship. Think about what most of us carry. Aadhaar proves identity and residence. A voter ID proves you are eligible to vote. A PAN card exists for taxation. A ration card determines welfare benefits. A birth certificate records where and when you were born. Every document proves something but by itself, none conclusively prove that you are an Indian citizen.That is partly because citizenship itself is not one-size-fits-all. If you were born in India before July 1, 1987, birth alone generally made you a citizen. Those born between 1987 and 2004 needed at least one parent to be an Indian citizen. Those born after December 3, 2004 face stricter conditions linked to their parents' citizenship and immigration status. Others become citizens through descent, registration or naturalisation. Your citizenship, therefore, depends not on a single document but on which provision of the Citizenship Act applies to you.Perhaps that explains why Indians have become chronic collectors of paperwork. School leaving certificates are never thrown away. Old electricity bills, land records, birth certificates and decades-old family papers are preserved because nobody knows which document might one day be demanded. Women often spend years updating records after marriage because names and addresses change. The Bombay High Court recognised this complexity in a 2013 judgment, observing that documents like passports, Aadhaar cards or birth certificates may establish different facts, but questions of citizenship ultimately have to be determined under the Citizenship Act.Legally, the government's position is not entirely new but perhaps that is the larger problem. The average citizen does not think in terms of Acts and legal provisions. They think in terms of trust. If the Government of India has scrutinised my documents before finally handed me a passport, why shouldn't I believe that it certifies I am an Indian citizen?The uncomfortable answer is that India still has no single, universally accepted proof of citizenship. Not Aadhaar. Not a voter ID. Not a PAN card. And, as we have now been reminded, not even a passport.Which leaves us with a question that should have had a simple answer long ago. If someone asked you today to conclusively prove that you are an Indian citizen, what one document would you produce?

title: হেপ্পী বাৰ্থডে ধৰ্মেন্দ্ৰ প্ৰধান, প্লীজ ৰিজাইনঃ ক'কৰোছ জনতা পাৰ্টী
Odisha Irrigation: କୋଦାଳ ଧରି ୨କିମି କେନାଲ ନିର୍ମାଣ କଲେ ଗ୍ରାମବାସୀ; ଚାଷଜମିରେ ହସିବ ଫସଲ
description:
ପାରଳାଖେମୁଣ୍ଡି (ସୁଜିତ ବିଷୋୟୀ): ସରକାରୀ ଅବହେଳା ମଧ୍ୟରେ ନିଜ ଚାଷଜମିକୁ ଜଳସେଚନ ସୁବିଧା ପହଞ୍ଚାଇବା ପାଇଁ ଶ୍ରମଦାନର ଅନନ୍ୟ ଉଦାହରଣ ସୃଷ୍ଟି କରିଛନ୍ତି ଗଜପତି ଜିଲ୍ଲାର ତିନିଟି ଗାଁର ଲୋକେ। ଦୀର୍ଘ ୧୨ ବର୍ଷ ଧରି କେନାଲ ନିର୍ମାଣ ଦାବି ସତ୍ତ୍ୱେ କୌଣସି ପଦକ୍ଷେପ ନ ନିଆଯିବାରୁ ଶେଷରେ ଗ୍ରାମବାସୀମାନେ ନିଜେ ହାତରେ କେନାଲ ଖନନ କାର୍ଯ୍ୟ ଆରମ୍ଭ କରିଛନ୍ତି। ନିଜର ଚାଷଜମିରେ ଫସଲ ଉତ୍ପାଦନ କରିବା ପାଇଁ ଗ୍ରାମବାସୀମାନେ ବଡ଼ ଉଦ୍ୟମ କରିଛନ୍ତି। ଏଭଳି ଦୃଶ୍ୟ ଦେଖିବାକୁ ମିଳିଛି ମୋହନା ବ୍ଲକର ଦୁର୍ଗମ ବିରିକୋଟ ଓ ଗୁଲୁବା ଗ୍ରାମ ପଞ୍ଚାୟତ ଅଞ୍ଚଳରେ। ଏହି ଦୁଇ ପଞ୍ଚାୟତ ଅନ୍ତର୍ଗତ ତେଲେଙ୍ଗାପଦା, ଡେଙ୍ଗିଲି ଓ କୁଡ଼ିକିମା ଗାଁର ପ୍ରାୟ ୨୦୦ ଏକର ଚାଷ ଜମି ଜଳସେଚନ ଅଭାବରୁ ବର୍ଷବର୍ଷ ଧରି ପଡ଼ିଆ ପଡ଼ି ରହିଥିଲା। ଏବେ ଏହି ଜମିରେ ଫସଲ ହସିବ ବୋଲି ଆଶା କରୁଛନ୍ତି। 
ସରକାରୀ ଅବହେଳା ସ୍ପଷ୍ଟ
ଗ୍ରାମବାସୀଙ୍କ ଦୀର୍ଘଦିନର ଦାବି ପରେ OTELP ଯୋଜନା ମାଧ୍ୟମରେ ଏକ ଚେକ୍ ଡ୍ୟାମ୍ ନିର୍ମାଣ କରାଯାଇଥିଲା। କିନ୍ତୁ ଚେକ୍ ଡ୍ୟାମରୁ ଚାଷ ଜମିକୁ ପାଣି ପହଞ୍ଚାଇବା ପାଇଁ ଆବଶ୍ୟକ କେନାଲ ନିର୍ମାଣ କରାଯାଇନଥିଲା। ଫଳରେ ଚେକ୍ ଡ୍ୟାମ ଥିଲେ ମଧ୍ୟ ଚାଷୀମାନେ ଏହାର ସୁଫଳ ପାଇପାରୁନଥିଲେ। ଏନେଇ ଗତ ୧୨ ବର୍ଷ ଧରି ଚାଷୀ ଓ ଗ୍ରାମବାସୀମାନେ ବିଭିନ୍ନ ସରକାରୀ କାର୍ଯ୍ୟାଳୟରେ ବାରମ୍ବାର ଦାବି ଜଣାଇଥିଲେ। ତଥାପି ସମସ୍ୟାର ସମାଧାନ ହୋଇନଥିଲା।
ସରକାର ଚେକ୍ ଡ୍ୟାମ ନିର୍ମାଣ କରିଥିବା ବେଳେ କେନାଲ ନିର୍ମାଣକୁ ଅଣଦେଖା କରିବାକୁ ନେଇ ସ୍ଥାନୀୟ ଲୋକଙ୍କ ମଧ୍ୟରେ ଅସନ୍ତୋଷ ଦେଖାଦେଇଥିଲା। ଶେଷରେ ବାଧ୍ୟ ହୋଇ ତେଲେଙ୍ଗାପଦା, ଡେଙ୍ଗିଲି ଓ କୁଡ଼ିକିମା ଗ୍ରାମର ଲୋକେ ନିଜ ଶ୍ରମ ଦାନ କରି ପ୍ରାୟ ଦୁଇ କିଲୋମିଟର ଲମ୍ବା କେନାଲ ନିର୍ମାଣ କାର୍ଯ୍ୟ ଆରମ୍ଭ କରିଛନ୍ତି। ପ୍ରାୟ ଏକ ମାସ ଧରି ଏହି କାମ ଜାରି ରହିଛି। ଗ୍ରାମବାସୀଙ୍କ ଆଶା, କେନାଲ ନିର୍ମାଣ ସମ୍ପୂର୍ଣ୍ଣ ହେଲେ ୨୦୦ ଏକରରୁ ଅଧିକ ଚାଷ ଜମିକୁ ପାଣି ପହଞ୍ଚିବ ଏବଂ ଦୀର୍ଘଦିନ ଧରି ପଡ଼ିଆ ପଡ଼ିଥିବା ଜମିରେ ପୁଣି ଚାଷ କାର୍ଯ୍ୟ ସମ୍ଭବ ହେବ। ଗ୍ରାମବାସୀଙ୍କ ଏହି ଉଦ୍ୟମ ଏକପଟେ ସେମାନଙ୍କର ଏକତା ଓ ସଂକଳ୍ପକୁ ପ୍ରମାଣିତ କରୁଥିବା ବେଳେ ଅନ୍ୟପଟେ ଗ୍ରାମାଞ୍ଚଳରେ ଜଳସେଚନ ଭିତ୍ତିଭୂମି ନିର୍ମାଣରେ ସରକାରୀ ଅବହେଳାକୁ ପୁଣିଥରେ ପଦାରେ ପକାଇଛି।
Tamilnadu: ତାମିଲନାଡୁ ଗ୍ୟାସ ଟ୍ରାଜେଡି ମୃତ୍ୟୁ ସଂଖ୍ୟା ୧୦କୁ ବୃଦ୍ଧି

title: 8th Pay Commission fitment factor to remain 2.57? Panel to stay cautious; how much central govt employees could get – Check Calculation
description: 8th Pay Commission arrears: Central government employees are eagerly awaiting the recommendations of the 8th Pay Commission. The discussions and meetings continue, and recommendations haven't been finalised yet. Here's what panels are cautious about.
content: 8th Pay Commission Latest News: The final deadline for central government employee unions, defence federations, and pensioners' associations to submit their feedback and memorandums to the 8th Central Pay Commission (CPC) ended on June 15, 2026. The commission officially made this the final cut-off, meaning no further extensions will be granted. 8th Pay Commission salary hike: Will fitment factor remain 2.57?While the Commission has yet to finalise its recommendations, early indications suggest that the fitment factor is likely to remain close to the 2.57 multiplier adopted under the Seventh Pay Commission. However, employee unions continue to press for a higher revision.Read more: Good News for Central Employees: 8th Pay Commission may bring Rs 14 lakh arrears, check full salary calculation 8th Pay Commission arrears: Current salary for Central Government employeesCurrently, Central Government employees and pensioners are paid under the 7th Pay Commission. As of now, the minimum basic pay for central government employees is Rs 18,000, while pensioners receive a minimum basic pension of Rs 9,000. Meanwhile, the maximum basic salary under the 7th Pay Commission is Rs 2,25,000, while apex positions like the Cabinet Secretary and others receive Rs 2,50,000 per month. Under 7 CPC, fitment was set at 2.57. Moreover, the DA/DR currently stands at 58 per cent.8th Pay Commission latest news: What is proposed fitment factor range for 8th Pay Commission?Staff Side of the National Council Joint Consultative Machinery (NC-JCM) has submitted a detailed 51-page memorandum to the 8th Pay Commission on the salary hike, outlining several key demands, a move that could cheer 1.2 crore central government employees and pensioners.One of these demands includes a massive fitment factor of 3.833 and a minimum basic salary of Rs 69,000. At this fitment factor, salaries of the central government employees and pensioners could see a significant jump of 283 per cent.8th Pay Commission arrears calculator: Estimates for Level 15-18 Central Government EmployeesAs the implementation timeline of the 8th Pay Commission salary hike recommendations remains uncertain, arrear calculations are being estimated based on a 20-month delay period, spanning from January 2026 to August 2027. This assumption stems from the Commission's 18-month deadline to submit its report, beginning in November 2025.In addition, experts expect the government may require another three to six months to review and implement the recommendations after the report is submitted.For each month of arrears, the payout will be equal to the difference between the basic pay in the 7th and the 8t 8th Pay Commissions. It will be multiplied by the fitment factor and the number of months (20) of report delay.Arrear of an employee = (Basic pay of employee in 8th CPC-basic pay of employee in 7th CPC) X fitment factor X number of months (20)The arrears will be calculated at fitment factors of 2.57 here, as commissions' early discussions suggest that the fitment factor could remain close to the 2.57 multiplier adopted by the Seventh Pay Commission.8th Pay Commission arrears calculator: 8th Pay Commission fitment factor 2.57According to the estimates based on a 2.57 fitment factor under the 8th Pay Commission, Level 15 employees could see their basic pay increase from Rs 1,82,200 to Rs 4,68,254, resulting in estimated arrears of Rs 57.21 lakh for 20 months. Level 16 employees may get arrears of Rs 64.50 lakh, with their basic pay rising from Rs 2,05,400 to Rs 5,27,878.For Level 17 employees, the revised basic pay could increase from Rs 2,25,000 to Rs 5,78,250, leading to arrears of Rs 70.65 lakh.Level 18 employees may see their basic pay rise in the 8th Pay Commission salary hike from Rs 2,50,000 to Rs 6,42,500, with estimated arrears of Rs 78.50 lakh for 20 months.Who are Level 15-18 central government employees?According to the 7th Pay Commission pay matrix, Level 15-18 employees belong to the Higher Administrative Grade (HAG) and above. These officers generally have around 30-35 years of service experience and belong to top-tier services such as the Indian Administrative Service (IAS), Indian Police Service (IPS), Indian Foreign Service (IFS), defence services and other senior government cadres.Level 15-18 officers occupy some of the highest positions in the government hierarchy. These include Secretaries, Additional Secretaries, Special Secretaries, Chief of Defence Staff, Director Generals in certain departments and other senior administrative and defence positions.Also read: 8th Pay Commission Salary Hike: How arrears for senior central govt employees could touch Rs 93 lakh? See calculations

title: CDSL Dividend 2026: Record date fixed for Rs 12.75 cash reward; check share price, dividend history
description: CDSL Final Dividend 2026: CDSL has fixed July 17, 2026, as the record date for its FY26 final dividend of Rs 12.75 per share. Eligible shareholders on the record date will receive the dividend, subject to AGM approval.
content: CDSL Final Dividend: Central Depository Services Limited (CDSL) on Thursday (June 25) announced the record date for its final dividend for the financial year 2025-26.CDSL Final Dividend 2026Earlier, the company's Board of Directors recommended a final dividend of Rs 12.75 per equity share of face value Rs 10, subject to shareholders' approval at the company's 28th Annual General Meeting (AGM). The dividend represents a 127.5 per cent payout on the face value of each share.In an exchange filing, the company said, "The Board of Directors of the Company, at its meeting held on May 2, 2026, had recommended a final dividend for the financial year 2025–26 of Rs 12.75 (Rupees Twelve and Seventy-Five Paise Only) per equity share of face value Rs 10, for approval by the shareholders at the Twenty-Eighth (28th) Annual General Meeting (AGM)."Eligible shareholders will receive Rs 12.75 for every equity share they hold as of the record date. For example, an investor holding 100 CDSL shares on the record date would be eligible to receive a total dividend of Rs 1,275, subject to approval at the AGM and applicable tax provisions.CDSL Dividend Record DateCDSL has fixed Friday, July 17, 2026, as the record date for determining shareholders eligible to receive the FY26 final dividend.The company said, "In terms of Regulation 42 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, the Company has fixed Friday, July 17, 2026, as the 'Record Date' for the purpose of determining the shareholders eligible to receive the dividend for the financial year 2025–26."The record date is the date on which a company determines the list of shareholders eligible to receive the declared dividend.CDSL Dividend HistoryCDSL has a strong dividend track record. Since 2020, the company has steadily increased its payouts, with dividends rising from Rs 4.50 per share to Rs 19 per share (including a Rs 3 special dividend) before declaring Rs 12.50 per share for FY25. The company also rewarded shareholders with a 1:1 bonus issue in 2024.CDSL Share PriceThe shares of the company, which has a market capitalisation of Rs 27,525.30 crore, settled 1.1%, or Rs 14.70, lower at Rs 1,317.(Disclaimer: The above article is meant for informational purposes only, and should not be considered as any investment advice. ET NOW DIGITAL suggests its readers/audience to consult their financial advisors before making any money-related decisions.)

title: Mehbooba Mufti Pays Tribute to Imam Hussain (A.S), Says Karbala Inspires Resistance Against Injustice
description:
Srinagar, June 26: PDP president Mehbooba Mufti paid tributes to Hazrat Imam Hussain (A.S.) and his companions on the occasion of Muharram, describing their sacrifice at Karbala as an enduring symbol of truth, justice, dignity and resistance against tyranny.
In a post on X, Mehbooba Mufti wrote, "On this solemn occasion of Muharram, I pay tribute to Imam Hussain (A.S.) and his noble companions, whose unparalleled sacrifice at Karbala remains an eternal beacon of truth, justice, dignity, and resistance against tyranny. Their message reminds humanity that silence in the face of injustice is a betrayal of our moral conscience."
"As people across the world continue to endure oppression, the spirit of Karbala inspires hope, resilience, and the courage to stand for what is right. Many see this same spirit reflected in Iran’s resolve during its recent conflict with the United States and Israel,"the post reads.

title: Microsoft Xbox Series S/X Console Prices Hiked Globally By Up to $150; 2TB Storage Variant Gets the Axe
description: Microsoft plans to sell refurbished Xbox Series S/X consoles
content: Microsoft on Thursday announced that starting August 1, the Xbox Series S/X consoles will cost more globally. The US-based tech giant has significantly raised the prices of both consoles, increasing the cost of the 512GB models by $100 (roughly Rs. 9,500). On the other hand, starting August 1, the 1TB models of both consoles will sell at a premium of a whopping $150 (about Rs. 14,000). Interestingly, while raising the prices of the other two variants, the tech giant discontinued the top-of-the-line 2TB option.

title: FIFA World Cup 2026 Breaks Historic Attendance Record Despite US Travel Chaos
description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup has set a new attendance record, surpassing the previous mark from 1994, with over 3.6 million spectators recorded after 56 matches.
content: The record which was set when the United States previously hosted the FIFA World Cup in 1994, has finally been surpassed, making the 2026 edition the most attended edition of the tournament in history. However, the milestone coincides with mounting criticism of the tournament's organisational problems, visa restrictions, and skyrocketing ticket prices. FIFA confirmed the new record at Germany's Group E encounter against Ecuador at MetLife Stadium, when attendance surpassed 3,605,357 following the first 56 games. The previous record of 3.587 million spectators set at the USA 1994 was surpassed.The demand for football has not decreased despite the tournament's growth from 52 games in 1994 to 104 this year. With 48 games remaining, stadium occupancy has averaged over 99 percent, and the ultimate attendance tally is predicted to surpass four million.Mounting Criticism Of 2026 World CupThe World Cup has been the target of criticism off the pitch for weeks, but the atmosphere inside the stadiums has seldom ever mirrored that criticism. Despite the off-field ruckus, fans have continued to fill stadiums, making every big game a spectacle.Ecuador picked up an upset win over Germany to advance to the World Cup knockout stages in front of another sold-out crowd of over 80,000 people in New Jersey. More than 281,000 spectators watched four matches throughout the three host countries on June 16, which also set a single-day attendance record earlier in the competition.Concerns about the tournament have not been allayed despite the high turnout.Many traditional fans were priced out by FIFA's dynamic pricing strategy, which drove ticket costs for a number of high-profile games into the hundreds of dollars. Fans from several nations, especially those in Africa and the Middle East, reportedly found it difficult to obtain authorisation to attend due to stricter US visa regulations and travel restrictions.Additionally, several host cities failed to experience the tourism boom they had hoped for as rising accommodation costs deterred tourists from staying longer.

Mahesh Dixit appointed Director of Intelligence Bureau
description:
Srinagar, June 26: The Centre has appointed senior IPS officer Mahesh Dixit as the new Director of the Intelligence Bureau (IB), the country's premier internal intelligence agency.
A 1993-batch IPS officer of the Telangana cadre, Dixit has extensive experience in intelligence operations, including a significant stint as head of the Subsidiary Intelligence Bureau (SIB) in Srinagar, where he supervised intelligence activities across Jammu, Kashmir and Leh.
During his tenure in the Valley, particularly in the aftermath of the abrogation of Article 370 in August 2019, Dixit was closely associated with intelligence gathering, security coordination and monitoring of the evolving internal security landscape.

title: Venezuela twin earthquakes: Death toll tops 230, over 41000 missing - RESCUE operations underway
description: Venezuela twin earthquakes: The powerful 7.2 and 7.5 earthquakes struck within a minute of each other on Wednesday evening, toppling buildings and severely damaging the country’s main international airport. At least, 4,300 people have been reportedly injured so far.
content: Venezuela twin earthquakes: Rescue teams continue to desperately search for survivors across Venezuela after two powerful earthquakes had struck within just 39 seconds of each other, triggering building collapses, road damage and tsunami alerts across the Caribbean region.Death toll mountsThe death toll from twin earthquakes that rocked Venezuela rose to 235 with thousands more injured, Bloomberg reported. A Reuters report, citing a website set up to track missing people and shared by leaders from the country’s opposition, listed more than 41,000 people as unaccounted for just after 3:30 pm (1930 GMT). However, Reuters could not verify the reports.Twin earthquakes strike VenezuelaThe powerful 7.2 and 7.5 earthquakes struck within a minute of each other on Wednesday evening, toppling buildings and severely damaging the country’s main international airport. People across the country were frantically searching for missing relatives, with social media filling with photographs and appeals for information.4,300  people injured“We’re carrying out an extraordinary effort across the country,” Health Minister Carlos Alvarado told state television. “Hospitals in the La Guaira region, the worst-hit region, are at maximum capacity and we’ve started to set up field hospitals.” He said 4,300 people have been injured along with 235 killed.Interim president Delcy Rodriguez earlier declared La Guaira a disaster zone, where more than 250 buildings were damaged. The coastal region borders the capital Caracas and houses the country’s Simón Bolívar International Airport, which suffered extensive damage. At least five other Venezuelan states have been impacted.Many places were experiencing outages of electricity, water and gas supply amid continued aftershocks. Venezuela’s oil facilities, however, appeared to be functioning normally, though one loading terminal near the epicenter was damaged.By Thursday afternoon, planes with help from the US, Spain, Mexico, Qatar and the UN had already departed toward Venezuela. Rescue teams also expected to begin arriving from countries including Colombia, Panama, Dominican Republic, and El Salvador. Rodriguez said China, Brazil and Caribbean countries have also pledged aid.Government plans reconstruction fundThe government will create an initial USD 200 million reconstruction fund using International Monetary Fund resources to finance repairs to infrastructure, hospitals and housing, Rodríguez said on state television. Officials are also discussing an additional fund to support victims, while public and private banks will activate special credit lines for people who lost businesses or jobs.The fund will “allow us to rebuild infrastructure, hospitals, and build homes for those that lost theirs,” Rodríguez said in a Thursday morning report, adding that the government would lease private-sector machinery to support rescue operations.President Donald Trump said the US “stands ready, willing, and able” to help. “I have instructed all agencies of our government to get ready to move quickly,” he said late Wednesday on social media.The US Treasury Department on Thursday issued a license authorizing all transactions related to earthquake relief efforts in Venezuela that would otherwise be prohibited by sanctions.(With Bloomberg inputs)

title: মুখ্যমন্ত্ৰীক ফোন কৰি কি ক’লে জাপানৰ ৰাষ্ট্ৰদূতে...
description:
ডিজিটেল ডেস্ক : জাপানৰ প্ৰধানমন্ত্ৰী ছানায়ে টাকাইচি অসমলৈ নাহিলেও মুখ্যমন্ত্ৰী ড০ হিমন্ত বিশ্ব শৰ্মালৈ আহিল এটা ফোন। এই ফোনটো কৰিছিল নতুন দিল্লীত থকা জাপানৰ ৰাষ্ট্ৰদূত কেঈচি অ’নোৱে। 
মুখ্যমন্ত্ৰীক জাপানৰ ৰাষ্ট্ৰদূতগৰাকীয়ে এক্ট- ইষ্ট পলিচিৰ অধীনত অসমৰ সৈতে জাপান চৰকাৰৰ সহযোগিতা আৰু অংশীদাৰিত্ব সুদৃঢ় কৰাৰ প্ৰতিশ্ৰুতি প্ৰদান কৰে। মুখ্যমন্ত্ৰী ড০ হিমন্ত বিশ্ব শৰ্মাই সদৰী কৰা মতে, ইলেক্ট্ৰনিক্স ছেমিকণ্ডাক্টৰ আৰু উৎপাদন তথা উদ্যোগ আৰু উদ্যোমিতা ক্ষেত্ৰখনত পাৰস্পৰিক সহযোগিতাৰ ক্ষেত্ৰত সহমত পোষণ কৰে তেওঁ।
এই সন্দৰ্ভত মুখ্যমন্ত্ৰীয়ে সামাজিক মাধ্যমত সদৰী কৰিছে এইদৰে- জাপানৰ মাননীয় ৰাষ্ট্ৰদূত কেঈচি অ’নো ডাঙৰীয়াৰ সৈতে কালি ফোনযোগে এক আন্তৰিক আৰু ফলপ্ৰসূ বাৰ্তালাপ কৰোঁ। আমাৰ এই আলোচনা কালত তেখেতে ভাৰতৰ এক্ট ইষ্ট পলিচীৰ অধীনত অসমৰ সৈতে জাপান চৰকাৰৰ চলি থকা অংশীদাৰিত্ব আৰু সহযোগিতা অধিক সুদৃঢ় কৰাৰ প্ৰতিশ্ৰুতি পুনৰ ব্যক্ত কৰে।
ইলেক্ট্ৰনিক্স, ছেমিকণ্ডাক্টৰ, আন্তঃগাঁথনি, উৎপাদন উদ্যোগ আৰু উদ্যমিতা আদি গুৰুত্বপূৰ্ণ ক্ষেত্ৰত পাৰস্পৰিক সহযোগিতা অধিক বিস্তৃত, গতিশীল আৰু ফলপ্ৰসূ কৰাৰ ক্ষেত্ৰত আমি উমৈহতীয়াভাৱে সহমত প্ৰকাশ কৰোঁ। অনাগত দিনসমূহত অসমৰ জনসাধাৰণৰ বাবে অধিক বিনিয়োগ, উদ্যোগ আৰু অৰ্থনৈতিক সুযোগ সৃষ্টি কৰাৰ লক্ষ্যৰে জাপানৰ উদ্যোগ আৰু প্ৰতিষ্ঠানসমূহৰ সৈতে অধিক নিবিড় সংযোগ আৰু সহযোগিতা বৃদ্ধি কৰিবলৈ অসম সদায়েই আশাবাদী।

title: Oppo Reno 16F, Reno 16, Reno 16 Pro Launched With 50-Megapixel Cameras, 80W Fast Charging: Price, Specifications
description: Oppo Reno 16 Pro runs on a MediaTek Dimensity 8550 Super SoC
content: After launching the Reno 16 series in China, Oppo has launched the phones in global markets. The series includes Reno 16, Reno 16 Pro, and Reno 16F. Both the Reno 16 and Reno 16 Pro are equipped with a 6,000mAh battery that supports 80W fast charging. The Reno 16F features a 7,000mAh cell with the same charging support. The trio offers IP69K ratings for resistance against dust and water and is pre-installed with ColorOS 16.

title: Uttarakhand: ଉତ୍ତରାଖଣ୍ଡରେ ନିହାଙ୍ଗ ଶିଖ୍‌ଙ୍କ ବିରୋଧ ଥମିଲା: ଡେରାଡୁନ୍‌ ସ୍ଥିତି ସ୍ବାଭାବିକ
description:
ଡେରାଡୁନ: ନାଗରାସୁ ଓ କର୍ଣ୍ଣପ୍ରୟାଗ ଗୁରୁଦ୍ୱାର ବିବାଦକୁ ନେଇ ନିହାଙ୍ଗ ଶିଖ୍‌ ଓ ଉତ୍ତରାଖଣ୍ଡ ପୁଲିସ ମଧ୍ୟରେ ଦେଖା ଦେଇଥିବା ଉତ୍ତେଜନା ଶେଷରେ ଥମିଛି। କର୍ଣ୍ଣପ୍ରୟାଗ ଓ ନାଗରାସୁ ଗୁରୁଦ୍ୱାରରେ ଏକ ବିବାଦକୁ ନେଇ ପୁଲିସ ଚାରି ଜଣ ନିହାଙ୍ଗ ଶିଖଙ୍କୁ ଗିରଫ କରିଥିଲା। ଏହି ଗିରଫଦାରୀ ପ୍ରତିବାଦରେ ନିହାଙ୍ଗମାନଙ୍କର ଏକ ଗୋଷ୍ଠୀ ହିମାଚଳ-ଉତ୍ତରାଖଣ୍ଡ ସୀମାରେ ଥିବା କୁଲହଲ ଚେକପୋଷ୍ଟରେ ପହଞ୍ଚିଥିଲା। ପାଓଣ୍ଟା ସାହିବରେ ପ୍ରଶାସନ ସହିତ ଆଲୋଚନା କରାଯାଇଥିଲା, କିନ୍ତୁ କୌଣସି ଚୁକ୍ତି ହୋଇନଥିବାରୁ ନିହାଙ୍ଗମାନେ ଆଗକୁ ବଢ଼ିଥିଲେ। ଉତ୍ତରାଖଣ୍ଡ ପୁଲିସ ସହିତ ସାରା ରାତି ଚାଲିଥିବା ଆଲୋଚନା ପରେ ନିହାଙ୍ଗମାନେ ଶୁକ୍ରବାର ଭୋର ୩ଟା ୩୦ରେ ଫେରିବାକୁ ରାଜି ହୋଇଥିଲେ। ଏହାସହିତ ଡେରାଡୁନରେ ପରିସ୍ଥିତି ସ୍ୱାଭାବିକ ହେବାରେ ଲାଗିଛି। 
ଶାନ୍ତିପୂର୍ଣ୍ଣ ସମାଧାନ ଉଦ୍ଦେଶ୍ୟ
କୁଲହାଲ ସୀମାରେ ଆରମ୍ଭ ହୋଇଥିବା ଏହି ପ୍ରସଙ୍ଗ ଡେରାଡୁନ ଓ ଋଷିକେଶକୁ ବ୍ୟାପି ଯାଇଥିଲା, ଯାହାଫଳରେ ଅନେକ ସ୍ଥାନରେ ବ୍ୟାରିକେଡ୍ ବ୍ୟବସ୍ଥା କରାଯାଇଥିଲା। ସହରରେ ପ୍ରବଳ ଟ୍ରାଫିକ୍ ଜାମ୍ ହୋଇଥିଲା।ଗୁରୁବାର ନିହାଙ୍ଗମାନେ ଘୋଷଣା କରିଥିଲେ ଯେ, ସେମାନେ ପଞ୍ଜାବ ଓ ହିମାଚଳ ପ୍ରଦେଶରୁ ଉତ୍ତରାଖଣ୍ଡ ଅଭିମୁଖେ ପଦଯାତ୍ରା କରିବେ। ପ୍ରତିବାଦରେ ନିହାଙ୍ଗମାନଙ୍କର ଏକ ଗୋଷ୍ଠୀ ହିମାଚଳ-ଉତ୍ତରାଖଣ୍ଡ ସୀମାରେ ଥିବା କୁଲହଲ ଚେକପୋଷ୍ଟରେ ପହଞ୍ଚିଥିଲା। ପାଓଣ୍ଟା ସାହିବରେ ପ୍ରଶାସନ ସହିତ ଆଲୋଚନା କରାଯାଇଥିଲା, କିନ୍ତୁ ପଦଯାତ୍ରା ବନ୍ଦ କରିବା ପାଇଁ କୌଣସି ଚୁକ୍ତି ହୋଇନଥିଲା, ଯାହା ପରେ ନିହାଙ୍ଗମାନେ ଆଗକୁ ବଢ଼ିଥିଲେ।
ଆଇନ କାନୁନ ପରିସ୍ଥିତି ସୃଷ୍ଟି କରିବେ ନାହିଁ ବୋଲି ନିହଙ୍ଗା ଗୋଷ୍ଠୀ କହିଥିଲା। ତେବେ ଆଲୋଚନା ସଫଳ ହୋଇନଥିବାରୁ କର୍ତ୍ତୃପକ୍ଷ ସୀମାରେ ହାଇଆଲର୍ଟ ଜାରି କରି ବହୁ ସଂଖ୍ୟକ ପୁଲିସ ଫୋର୍ସ ମୁତୟନ କରିଥିଲେ ଓ ଉତ୍ତେଜନାକୁ ରୋକିବା ପାଇଁ ବ୍ୟାରିକେଡ୍ ନିର୍ମାଣ କରିଥିଲେ। ପ୍ରତିନିଧିମଣ୍ଡଳରେ ସାମିଲ ଥିବା କଂଗ୍ରେସ ନେତା ଓ ଉତ୍ତରାଖଣ୍ଡ ଶିଖ ସମ୍ପ୍ରଦାୟର ପ୍ରତିନିଧି ଅମରଜିତ ସିଂହ କହିଛନ୍ତି ଯେ, ନିହଙ୍ଗମାନଙ୍କର ଉଦ୍ଦେଶ୍ୟ କେବେବି ଅଶାନ୍ତି ସୃଷ୍ଟି କରିବା ନୁହେଁ ବରଂ ଶାନ୍ତିପୂର୍ଣ୍ଣ ଭାବରେ ସମସ୍ୟାର ସମାଧାନ କରିବା। ସିଂହ ଏହି ଅଚଳାବସ୍ଥା ସମାଧାନରେ ଗୁରୁତ୍ୱପୂର୍ଣ୍ଣ ଭୂମିକା ଗ୍ରହଣ କରିବା ପାଇଁ ପ୍ରଶାସନକୁ ଶ୍ରେୟ ଦେଇଥିଲେ କିନ୍ତୁ ସରକାରଙ୍କ ପକ୍ଷରୁ କିଛି ତ୍ରୁଟି ମଧ୍ୟ ରହିଛି ବୋଲି କହିଥିଲେ। ଡେରାଡୁନ ସ୍ୱାଭାବିକ ଅବସ୍ଥା ଫେରିବା ପରେ ମଧ୍ୟ ପୁଲିସ ସତର୍କତା ଭାବେ ଋଷିକେଶ ସମେତ ଅନେକ ପ୍ରବେଶ ସ୍ଥାନରେ ଯାଞ୍ଚ ଓ ବ୍ୟାରିକେଡିଂ ଜାରି ରଖିଛି। ଉତ୍ତରାଖଣ୍ଡରେ ବର୍ତ୍ତମାନ ଚାର୍‌ଧାମ୍‌ ଯାତ୍ରା ଓ ହେମକୁଣ୍ଡ ସାହେବ ତୀର୍ଥଯାତ୍ରା ପାଇଁ ଲକ୍ଷ ଲକ୍ଷ ତୀର୍ଥଯାତ୍ରୀ ଉପସ୍ଥିତ ଥିବାବେଳେ କଡ଼ା ସୁରକ୍ଷା ବ୍ୟବସ୍ଥା ଗ୍ରହଣ କରାଯାଇଛି।
Strait Of Hormuz: ହର୍ମୁଜ ପ୍ରଣାଳୀରେ ମାଲବାହୀ ଜାହାଜ ଉପରେ ଇରାନର ଆକ୍ରମଣ

title: Nashik Crime News: जुन्या वादातून देवळाली गावात युवकाचा खून; संशयिताला पोलिसांकडून अटक
description: नाशिकरोड | प्रतिनिधीदेवळाली गाव येथे भल्या पहाटे आज शुक्रवार दिनांक 26 रोजी जुन्या वादातून एका युवकाचा खून करण्यात आल्याची घटना घडली असून या प्रकरणी संशयित आरोपीला उपनगर पोलिसांनी अटक केली आहे. या घटनेमुळे देवळाली गाव व परिसरात खळबळ उडाली असून पोलीस त्वरित घटनास्थळी दाखल झाले. ‎मिळालेल्या माहितीनुसार, विशाल माळवे (वय ३६, रा. सुवर्ण सोसायटी, खोले […]
content:
नाशिकरोड | प्रतिनिधीदेवळाली गाव येथे भल्या पहाटे आज शुक्रवार दिनांक 26 रोजी जुन्या वादातून एका युवकाचा खून करण्यात आल्याची घटना घडली असून या प्रकरणी संशयित आरोपीला उपनगर पोलिसांनी अटक केली आहे. या घटनेमुळे देवळाली गाव व परिसरात खळबळ उडाली असून पोलीस त्वरित घटनास्थळी दाखल झाले.



‎मिळालेल्या माहितीनुसार, विशाल माळवे (वय ३६, रा. सुवर्ण सोसायटी, खोले मळा) असे खून झालेल्या युवकाचे नाव असून शुक्रवारी पहाटे विशाल माळवे याचा खून करण्यात आला. मयत विशाल माळवे आणि त्याचा मित्र अमोल गायकवाड रात्री एकत्र पार्टी करीत असताना दोघांमध्ये जुन्या वादातून वादावादी झाली. या वादाचे रूपांतर हाणामारीत होऊन संशयित अमोल गायकवाड याने धारदार हत्याराने विशाल माळवे याच्यावर वार केले. या हल्ल्यात गंभीर जखमी झालेल्या विशालचा मृत्यू झाला.



संपादकीय : २६ जून २०२६ – एवढी हिंमत येते तरी कुठून?



‎घटनेची माहिती मिळताच उपनगर पोलिस घटनास्थळी दाखल झाले. पोलिसांनी मृतदेह ताब्यात घेऊन शवविच्छेदनासाठी जिल्हा रुग्णालयात पाठविला आहे. दरम्यान, संशयित अमोल गायकवाड याला पोलिसांनी ताब्यात घेतले असून, उपनगर पोलिस ठाण्यात गुन्हा दाखल करण्याचे काम सुरू आहे.



‎काही दिवसांपूर्वी तपोवन परिसरात सुटकेसमध्ये महिलेचा मृतदेह आणि कामगार कॉलनीतील इमारतीच्या टेरेसवर युवकाचा मृतदेह आढळल्याच्या घटना ताज्या असतानाच आणखी एका खुनाच्या घटनेने नाशिककरांमध्ये भीतीचे वातावरण पसरले आहे. दरम्यान खुनाची घटना घडल्यानंतर वरिष्ठ पोलिस निरीक्षक जयंत शिरसाठ व त्यांचे सहकारी तातडीने घटनास्थळी दाखल झाले याबाबत पुढील तपास उपनगर पोलिस करीत आहेत.

title: TaMo CV delay won't hit long-term gain: Bandyopadhyay
Underweight Deliveries, Heavy Questions As Consumers Raise Red Flags
description: Deceiving to sell: Small weight discrepancies, missing product details, and recurring consumer complaints expose growing transparency gaps in quick commerce, raising questions about accountability and fair business practices.
content: Deceiving to sell: Convenience has quietly become an addiction. Why step out for everyday essentials when a quick commerce app can drop them at your door in minutes? But in the rush to make life easier, there is something we are not noticing — in the name of convenience, we may be buying health hazards too.That is exactly why Times Now Digital is running a consumer rights protection campaign, asking quick commerce platforms to be fair and transparent with their customers. One of the most hazardous and unethical practices we are flagging is the failure to clearly display the manufacturing and expiry dates of perishable products, among other lapses. Times Now Digital reached out to Zepto, Blinkit, Instamart, Flipkart Minutes, and Amazon to understand why manufacturing and expiry dates are not displayed on their platforms. None of them responded. On a basic consumer-safety question — what's the manufacturing date? When does it expire? So far, there has been no response to these basic consumer-safety questions. The campaign's core focus is transparency — and a big part of transparency comes down to a simple test: what you ordered versus what you get.We got in touch with some loyal customers of quick commerce platforms to find out whether they had run into transparency issues, recently or in the past, and whether those problems kept recurring. One regular user pointed to what they described as another major concern.In a recent incident under our investigation, the customer from the Delhi NCR area ordered vegetables from Zepto. When the order arrived, they grew curious because the carrots looked lighter than expected. On weighing them, they found the quantity was lower than what was listed on the platform: the carrots were supposed to weigh between 200 and 300 grams, but the package came in at just 196 grams.Although the discrepancy was not large, it was enough to raise concern. The customer then weighed the other vegetable packs in the order, and to their surprise, every one weighed less than the quantity listed on the platform.According to the customer, a pack of onions listed at between 900 grams and 1 kilogram weighed 859 grams. A pack of tomatoes listed at 500 grams weighed 460 grams, while a cucumber pack listed at between 500 and 600 grams weighed 490 grams.                      While the differences may appear small on a single order, the customer questioned whether consumers are consistently receiving the quantities they pay for and whether greater transparency is needed from quick commerce platforms.Issue Raised With The Zepto Support TeamWhen the issue was raised with the platform, and after several exchanges of messages and images, another problem came to light: the customer was unable to upload images directly to the platform due to an error. This too was flagged to the support executive, which ultimately led the customer to email the images to the Zepto support team.                      After a long discussion lasting about 25 minutes, the company offered a refund of Rs 45. The customer repeatedly questioned how a delivery in which the weight of the vegetables did not match the weight listed on the website could be settled with a refund alone. Not everybody is going to weigh their vegetables every day, the customer argued, and so such discrepancies may go unnoticed. The customer further pointed out that the issue was not limited to a single packet of vegetables.                  Despite these concerns, the support team maintained that it could only provide a refund and said the matter would be escalated to “higher authorities”.This is not the first time a quick commerce customer has faced such an issue. Several people have come forward on social media with similar complaints about the weight of fruits and vegetables on other platforms like Swiggy Instamart and Blinkit, among others.                    Similar Incidents Against Quick Comms Raised In PastPreviously, one customer shared his frustration with Blinkit on Reddit, alleging that he had received significantly less than the quantity he had paid for. He shared an image of a grape order and claimed the package weighed less than the amount listed on the platform, adding that he had encountered similar issues with Blinkit deliveries before. The user claimed that a 500 gm pack of grapes weighed just 395 grams, including the packaging.          In another incident, a Reddit user claimed that Swiggy Instamart had delivered underweight vegetables, sharing photos and screenshots of conversations with customer support. According to the user, several items in the order weighed less than the quantities listed on the platform: a cauliflower listed at 400–600 grams weighed just 145 grams, while a 250-gram capsicum pack weighed 170 grams and a 1-kg potato order weighed 965 grams.                      Pls check your Blinkit deliveries byu/Jusklickin inindiaGovernment Mandates For Quick Commerce PlatformsThe growing number of complaints about underweight fruit and vegetable deliveries raises serious questions about transparency and accountability in the quick commerce sector. While individual discrepancies may seem minor, but consumers argue that even small shortfalls can add up over time. And these concerns are not new.For at least the past two years, regulators such as the Central Consumer Protection Authority (CCPA) and the Food Safety and Standards Authority of India (FSSAI) have urged e-commerce and quick commerce platforms to clearly display key consumer information, including expiry dates, accurate product weights and other essential details. Despite these directives, transparency-related issues continue to surface. The FSSAI recently said it had issued more than two dozen notices to e-commerce Food Business Operators (FBOs) in recent weeks.Yet many consumers still say they do not receive complete information, or the quantity they paid for, raising fresh questions about how effectively the rules are being enforced across the sector.A few grams of carrots. A Rs 45 refund. Easy enough to shrug off, until you multiply it across millions of orders, every single day. Convenience may be arriving at our doorstep in minutes, but the question worth weighing is this: how much are we quietly handing over in return?

title: PM Modi remembers sacrifice of Hazrat Imam Hussain on Muharram
description:
New Delhi, June 26: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Friday remembered the sacrifice of Hazrat Imam Hussain and said it continues to inspire many people to remain steadfast in the pursuit of truth and justice.
The prime minister conveyed the message on the occasion of Muharram.
"The sacrifice of Hazrat Imam Hussain (AS) continues to inspire many people to remain steadfast in the pursuit of truth and justice. It is also a reminder of the enduring power of courage and conviction," PM Modi said in a post on 'X'.
Muharram is observed by Shia Muslims to commemorate the martyrdom of Hazrat Imam Hussain, the grandson of Prophet Muhammad, at the Battle of Karbala.

title: Welcome To The Jungle Honest Review: Plenty of noise, a few laughs, but is this really the comedy we still want to 'Welcome'?
description: Welcome To The Jungle Review: Akshay Kumar's comedy delivers occasional laughs and clever meta jokes, but its reliance on mocking disabilities, ageing, body types and appearances feels outdated. Jacqueline Fernandez shines, while Disha Patani remains severely underutilised in overcrowded entertainer.
content: Welcome To The Jungle Honest Review: I laughed. Then I wondered why...There is no denying that Welcome To The Jungle wants to entertain. It is loud, unapologetically chaotic and packed with enough stars to make even the busiest casting director nervous. Every few minutes, someone walks in, someone shouts louder than the last person, another explosion follows and another joke is thrown at the audience. Sometimes it works brilliantly. Sometimes it crashes spectacularly. I laughed more than I expected to. There are moments that genuinely land, witty one-liners that catch you off guard and several self-aware jokes that show the film isn't entirely oblivious to its own madness. Yet, every time I found myself enjoying the humour, another joke arrived at someone else's expense. That is where Welcome To The Jungle begins to lose me.               When Humour Stops Being Clever For a film released in 2026, its idea of comedy often feels frozen in time. The screenplay repeatedly turns body types, ageing, speech defects, voice impairments, poor eyesight, accents and even language into easy punchlines. Instead of trusting clever writing, it chooses the quickest route to laughter. There is a noticeable difference between laughing with people and laughing at them. Unfortunately, the film often prefers the latter. The disappointing part is that this dependence on outdated humour isn't even necessary because the screenplay does contain genuinely funny situations. Had the writers relied more on wit than ridicule, Welcome To The Jungle could easily have been one of the franchise's strongest entertainers.Also Read: Can Akshay Kumar's Welcome To The Jungle revive Bollywood's lost multi-starrer comedy era? From Hera Pheri to Golmaal, why ensemble films still matter Leave Your Brain At Home Logic is perhaps the first casualty here. The story races from one absurd situation to another with little interest in coherence. But perhaps that is the point. This is a film that openly asks you to surrender to its madness. If you walk in expecting realism, you are in the wrong auditorium. If you are willing to switch off your brain for nearly three hours, the laughs become easier to find. Ahmed Khan keeps the pace relentless. There is barely a quiet moment as cameos, action, songs and comic set pieces continue arriving one after another. The energy rarely drops, even if the screenplay occasionally does. An Ensemble That Gives It Everything Welcome to the Jungle throws subtlety out of the window and embraces unfiltered madness from its opening scene. Packed with slapstick humour, nostalgic references and a cast so large it could populate an entire franchise, the film constantly aims for laughs. It certainly has boundless energy, but its scattershot storytelling means the jokes land inconsistently. For every genuinely funny moment, there are several that feel overextended or simply miss the mark.The plot is as absurd as the film itself. When a politician (Brijendra Kala) warns a wealthy businessman (Zakir Hussain) that a change in government could invite income tax raids, the latter comes up with an unusual solution—produce a lavish film to offset his tax burden. At the suggestion of his daughter (Jacqueline Fernandez), two struggling directors, Dev (Rajpal Yadav) and Das (Paresh Rawal), are hired, along with their visually impaired cameraman Nainsukh (Shreyas Talpade). They rope in washed-up movie star Raju (Akshay Kumar), who has been reduced to acting in low-budget Bhojpuri films, hoping he can revive both the film and his own career.Predictably, nothing goes according to plan. The production soon attracts two eccentric gangsters—Yeda Anna (Suniel Shetty), introduced as Uday Shetty's brother, and Romeo (Arshad Warsi), Majnu Bhai's sibling—who force themselves into the project with one demand: replace Raju as the hero. Actress Nadia (Disha Patani) joins the film but refuses to share screen space with her former boyfriend, while the already overflowing ensemble expands further with Yashpal Sharma, Mukesh Tiwari, Krushna Abhishek, Kiku Sharda, Tusshar Kapoor, Jackie Shroff, Lara Dutta, Farida Jalal, Kiran Kumar and several surprise cameos.Akshay Kumar pokes fun at his own box-office failures and even references his iconic Khiladi films, while Jackie Shroff's villain dismisses him with amusing indifference. The screenplay is filled with callbacks to Welcome, remixes of familiar songs, appearances by Daler Mehndi and actors from B.R. Chopra's Mahabharat, along with references to Bollywood classics and television nostalgia. These moments occasionally generate genuine smiles, but Farhad Samji's screenplay struggles to maintain consistency. Based on a story idea by the late Neeraj Vora, the film borrows heavily from the spirit of Tropic Thunder while attempting to parody everything from patriotic action films and CGI-heavy spectacles to celebrity culture, gangsters and the filmmaking business itself. Unfortunately, much of its humour also relies on outdated jokes about ageing, physical appearance, speech impairments, poor eyesight and accents, making several sequences feel more uncomfortable than funny. An overlong military training segment led by Lara Dutta and the climactic portions set in the border village of Azadpur stretch the comedy beyond its breaking point, while jokes involving Farida Jalal's mumbling elderly woman and Kiran Kumar's elaborate translations come across as insensitive rather than entertaining.If the film works at all, it is almost entirely because of its performers. Akshay Kumar looks like he is genuinely enjoying himself, and his willingness to poke fun at his own career is among the screenplay's smartest ideas. Suniel Shetty is an absolute scene-stealer, effortlessly commanding attention with his understated expressions and impeccable comic timing. Jacqueline Fernandez emerges as one of the film's biggest surprises, delivering several of its funniest one-liners with remarkable ease and proving she is more than capable in comedy. Rajpal Yadav once again demonstrates why he remains one of Bollywood's finest comic actors, elevating even average jokes through his impeccable timing and expressive reactions. Farida Jalal, despite limited screen time, steals almost every scene she appears in with her warmth and natural comic rhythm..               Paresh Rawal remains as dependable as ever, while Arshad Warsi slips comfortably into the franchise's madness with his trademark charm. Tusshar Kapoor continues to excel at physical comedy, and performers like Shreyas Talpade, Krushna Abhishek, Kiku Sharda, Mukesh Tiwari, Yashpal Sharma, Jackie Shroff and Lara Dutta commit wholeheartedly to the film's ridiculous premise. No one appears to be sleepwalking through their role; the cast gives the material far more conviction than the screenplay deserves.The only major casualty of this overcrowded narrative is Disha Patani. Her role is so underwritten that she is reduced largely to decorative presence, with little opportunity to display either her acting or comic abilities. In a film bursting with characters, it is disappointing that she is never given a single memorable moment.               The Writing Doesn't Match The Talent Ironically, the biggest problem isn't the actors. It is the screenplay. This is one of those rare films where almost everyone on screen appears committed to entertaining the audience, but the writing keeps letting them down. Instead of allowing situations and characters to generate humour organically, it repeatedly falls back on jokes that feel lazy and outdated. The film also leans heavily on nostalgia, revisiting familiar references and acknowledging its own cinematic legacy. Some callbacks work wonderfully and generate genuine smiles. Others feel like reminders of a time when Bollywood comedies relied more on memorable characters than endless chaos.               Verdict Welcome To The Jungle is neither consistently hilarious nor entirely forgettable. It delivers enough laughs to justify its existence, largely because of a cast that commits wholeheartedly to the madness. Akshay Kumar's self-aware humour works, Suniel Shetty is impossible to forget, Jacqueline Fernandez emerges as the surprise package, Rajpal Yadav once again demonstrates impeccable comic timing, and Farida Jalal quietly walks away with some of the film's best moments. But the film also exposes Bollywood's continued dependence on humour that punches down. Mocking disabilities, ageing, appearances and physical differences may have generated easy laughs once, but comedy has evolved and audiences have evolved with it. You will laugh. You will probably enjoy several moments. But as you leave the theatre, one question refuses to leave with you. If this is still Bollywood's definition of comedy, is this really the kind of Welcome we should be embracing?Cast: Akshay Kumar, Suniel Shetty, Tusshar Kapoor, Disha Patani, Jacqueline Fernandez, Arshad Warsi, Paresh Rawal, Lara Dutta, Rajpal Yadav, Shreyas Talpade and othersDirector: Ahmed KhanRating: ★⯨☆☆☆ (1.5/5)

title: Javid Ahmed Kitab suspended as JKCA President
description:
Srinagar, June 26: The Jammu and Kashmir Cricket Association (JKCA) has removed its President, Javid Ahmed Kitab, from performing his duties after the association's Apex Council approved the decision unanimously through a resolution passed by circulation, according to an official notification.
As per the notification, the resolution was circulated among the eligible members of the Apex Council under Rule 23(i) of the JKCA Constitution for consideration and approval. 
Eight members, out of the nine, communicated their approval of the resolution, while one member did not communicate any decision. Consequently, the resolution was deemed passed by an 8-0 majority in accordance with Rule 23(i) read with Rule 48(6) of the JKCA Constitution.
The notification stated that since the resolution pertained to the conduct and suspension of the President, it was not circulated to him. Instead, it was sent to the remaining nine members of the Apex Council.
"In terms of the said resolution, Sh. Javid Ahmed Kitab, President, Jammu & Kashmir Cricket Association, has been suspended with immediate effect," the notification reads.
It further stated that the resolution shall have the same force, validity and effect as if it had been passed during a duly convened meeting of the Apex Council and will be placed before the next meeting of the Council for ratification as required under the Constitution.
The JKCA has directed all officers, employees, affiliated units and functionaries of the association to take all consequential steps for implementation of the resolution forthwith in accordance with the Constitution of the association.

title: অবৈধ প্ৰেম কৰিব আহি ৰাইজৰ হাতত হ'ল লটি- ঘটি...
description:
অবৈধ প্ৰেম কৰিব আহি ৰাইজৰ হাতত লটি-ঘটি আনন্দ মালাকৰৰ।
নিশা হাজোৰ ভোমাৰবড়ীত তীব্ৰ উত্তেজনা।
হাজোৰ সাতদলা গাঁৱৰ বিবাহিত পুৰুষ আনন্দ মালাকৰৰ বিৰুদ্ধে উঠিছে এই অভিযোগ।
ভোমাৰবড়ী গাঁৱৰ এগৰাকী মহিলাৰ ঘৰত অবৈধ কামত লিপ্ত হৈ থকা সময়তে ৰাইজে হাতে লোটে কৰায়ত্ব কৰে মালাকৰক।
স্বামী নথকা এগৰাকী মহিলাৰ লগত আনন্দ মালাকৰৰ  দীৰ্ঘদিন অবৈধ প্ৰেমৰ সম্পৰ্ক থকাৰ কথা অভিযোগ স্থানীয় ৰাইজৰ। 
প্ৰতিদিনে মালাকৰে মহিলাগৰাকীৰ ঘৰত সোমাই  থকাৰ ফলত সামজিক পৰিৱেশ বিনষ্ট কৰাৰ অভিযোগ।
উপায়ন্তৰ হৈ গাঁওবাসীয়ে চৰিত্ৰহীন আনন্দ মালাকৰক অনৈতিক কাৰ্য্যত জড়িত হৈ থকাৰ সময়তে হাতে লোটে আটক কৰে।
পিছত আনন্দ মালাকৰক ৰাজহুৱা ফিল্ডত বহুৱাই ৰাখি হাজো আৰক্ষীক খবৰ দিয়ে।
ঘটনাস্থলীত হাজো আৰক্ষী উপস্থিত হৈ লম্পট আনন্দক ৰাইজৰ ৰোষৰ পৰা উদ্ধাৰ কৰে।
